summary: Markets face headwinds from deteriorating Iran ceasefire rhetoric and hotter-than-expected CPI inflation data, pressuring risk appetite despite some technical strength in crypto and energy. Geopolitical tensions keep oil elevated while precious metals consolidate, and traders await clarity on macro trends before committing to new positions.

signals.name: Bitcoin
signals.reason: DegenDave frames Bitcoin as still in a bullish run, expecting higher prices despite short‑term traps and CPI‑driven volatility.
signals.price_level: $85000 resistance mentioned as a target before a trap
signals.chart_pattern: bull flag / continuation pattern into resistance

signals.name: Solana
signals.reason: Title 'I'm Balls Deep in SOL' and prior ChartHackers‑style setups indicate strong long conviction into CPI‑driven volatility.
signals.price_level: near key resistance zone ahead of CPI

signals.reason: Title 'SOL & SUI Ready To Rip?' implies he expects a strong upside move in SUI alongside Solana.

signals.name: Gold
signals.reason: Daily and weekly VWAP both bearish; consolidating within parallel channel with key resistance at $5,000 psychological level; 50% Fibonacci retracement around $4,637.

signals.name: Brent Crude Oil
signals.reason: Brent bounced back above $100 to $101 (+3% on day) driven by Trump's comments on Iran ceasefire fragility and Strait of Hormuz risk premium.

signals.name: US Dollar vs Japanese Yen
signals.reason: Daily VWAP bullish despite weekly VWAP bearish; Japanese yen holds net short position of -61,738 contracts per CFTC.

signals.reason: Euro showing strength with net long position of 32,202 contracts per CFTC positioning data.

signals.reason: British pound holds significant net short position of -63,908 contracts, indicating sustained bearish positioning.

signals.reason: Mixed positioning with key levels at 6.8000 ($350m) and 6.7700 ($300m) reflecting consolidation amid macro uncertainty.

signals.name: Nvidia
signals.reason: Citi maintains buy rating with $300 price target ahead of May 20 earnings; projecting Q1 revenue of $80bn above consensus on strong Blackwell chip demand.

signals.name: ASYS (Semiconductor)
signals.reason: ASYS jumped 18.6% on strong earnings and upbeat forecast with YTD return of 65.7%, though high volatility noted.

signals.name: LASR (Laser/Tech)
signals.reason: LASR surged 11% on strong earnings and guidance with YTD return of 89.4%, indicating strong momentum but elevated volatility.

signals.name: EXOD
signals.reason: EXOD shifted to payments strategy and cut Bitcoin holdings by $100M+, with shares down 9.6% YTD reflecting strategic pivot concerns.

signals.name: Silver
signals.reason: Silver broke through $82 but pulled back today; significant overhead supply remains at $92-$93 resistance zone before bullish confirmation.
